page: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server featuring eight NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a total of 180 GB of VRAM. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores running at a base speed of 2.2 GHz. It includes 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25%. However, multi-core execution is highly efficient, with stress-ng multi-core performance and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, placing in the top 25% for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read operations. Most notably, the server excels in LLM inference, achieving top 10% tier performance for prompt processing across small, medium, and large models, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for medium models.

From a resource density perspective, the g2-standard-96 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized, GPU-bound workloads due to its dense allocation of eight L4 accelerators and strong memory bandwidth. However, a key tradeoff is its weak single-threaded performance and the lack of bundled local storage, which requires external storage solutions. This server is highly suited for large-scale LLM inference, multi-threaded graphics rendering, parallel data processing, and high-throughput web serving, while being less optimal for single-threaded applications or database workloads requiring high local IOPS.
description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server equipped with eight NVIDIA L4 GPUs delivering 180 GB of VRAM, alongside 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features a 2.2 GHz clock speed and lacks local storage. While single-core benchmarks are relatively weak, the server demonstrates strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth. It is highly optimized for parallel workloads, achieving top-tier benchmark results in large language model inference and multi-threaded processing. This hardware profile makes it an efficient choice for GPU-accelerated tasks, including AI inference, machine learning workloads, and high-throughput parallel computing, though it is less suited for single-threaded applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 is a graphics-optimized server with 8 NVIDIA L4 GPUs, 180 GB VRAM, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 384 GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform g2-standard-96 features 8 NVIDIA L4 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 384 GB RAM, optimized for high-performance LLM inference.
tagline: An eight-GPU accelerated platform delivering high-density parallel processing and top-tier large language model inference performance.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 180 GB of total VRAM
96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 384 GB of system memory
Top-tier benchmark performance for large language model inference
Strong multi-core processing and high memory bandwidth
No bundled local storage, requiring external storage configurations
Best suited for AI inference, parallel computing, and graphics rendering
